title: deploy-docker config (§5.4) -- mounted at spec.deploy in the composed schema

image.title: Image
image.description: Docker image to run, e.g. 'ghcr.io/kavitareader/kavita:latest'. Include a tag -- 'latest' is fine but pinning a version is safer for reproducible deploys.

cmd.title: Command
cmd.description: Overrides the image's default CMD. Leave empty to use the image's own entrypoint/command unchanged.

entrypoint.title: Entrypoint
entrypoint.description: Overrides the image's ENTRYPOINT. Rarely needed -- most images don't require this.

env.title: Environment variables
env.description: Plain (non-secret) environment variables passed to the container. For credentials/API keys, use the secrets store instead -- values here are visible in the desired-state files.

mounts.title: Mounts
mounts.description: Named volumes or host bind mounts attached to the container.

source.title: Host path
source.description: Absolute path on the host, e.g. '/data/kavita/config'

devices.title: Devices
devices.description: Host device paths to pass through, e.g. '/dev/dri' for hardware transcoding. Granting device access is a powerful action requiring approval.

networkMode.title: Network mode
networkMode.description: Docker network mode override. Leave unset for the normal per-service network; 'host' is a powerful action requiring approval.

ports.title: Container ports
ports.description: Ports the container listens on internally. This does not publish them on the host -- exposure to the WAN is configured via spec.expose, not here.

capAdd.title: Added Linux capabilities
capAdd.description: Extra Linux capabilities to grant the container (e.g. 'NET_ADMIN'). Broadening capabilities is a powerful action requiring approval.

privileged.title: Privileged
privileged.description: Runs the container with full host privileges. Rarely needed and always a powerful action requiring approval.

user.title: Run as user
user.description: UID (or 'uid:gid') to run the container process as, overriding the image's default.

readOnly.title: Read-only root filesystem
readOnly.description: Mounts the container's own root filesystem read-only. Combine with explicit volume mounts for any paths the app needs to write to.

resources.title: Resource limits
resources.description: Memory and CPU limits for the container.

cpus.title: CPU limit
cpus.description: e.g. '1.5' for one and a half cores

labels.title: Container labels
labels.description: Arbitrary Docker labels attached to the container, e.g. for external tooling that reads container metadata.

restart.title: Restart policy
restart.description: Docker restart policy, e.g. 'unless-stopped' or 'on-failure'. Leave unset for the platform default.
